title: F'tral

{{Quote|The planet of the Iyra, F'tral, is a large world orbiting the blue-white star, F'la Ren.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
'''F'tral''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Waterworld/Legends|waterworld]] located in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]]. The [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface was almost completely covered by [[Water/Legends|freshwater]] [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]], with dry land being limited to chains of [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] islands that were home to mostly [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] or [[Parasite/Legends|parasitic]] [[Plant/Legends|plants]]. [[Cephalopod/Legends|Cephalopods]], including the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Iyra]], were the dominant form of life in F'tral's oceans.

The Iyra lived in underwater [[City/Legends|cities]] and [[Metallurgy/Legends|smelted]] [[Ore/Legends|ores]] using the [[Temperature/Legends|heat]] of undersea volcanoes, which allowed them to developed advanced technologies, such as corrosion-resistant alloys; hydraulic, [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]], and [[Nuclear fusion|fusion]] technologies; and [[Starship/Legends|starship]] construction. The latter allowed the Iyra to eventually become widespread in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as [[Merchant/Legends|traders]]. The [[sentientologist]] [[Tem Eliss]] [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from F'tral despite predominantly spending [[Gender/Legends|his]] youth offworld.

==Description==
===An Outer Rim waterworld===
{{Quote|Clear, fresh water covers 93 percent of the planet's surface. ''[…]'' The little land that does exist on F'tral is located in chains of volcanic islands that form where the planet's tectonic plates collide.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
F'tral,<ref name="GG42" /> the name of which meant "Water" in the [[Iyra language|language]] of<ref name="GG41" /> its [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Iyra]] [[Species/Legends|species]], was a large [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="GG42" /> located in the [[F'tral system/Legends|F'tral system]], a part of the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Color/Legends|blue]]-white [[Star/Legends|star]] [[F'la Ren]]. Ninety-three percent of the planet's surface was [[Waterworld/Legends|covered]] by [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of clear [[Water/Legends|freshwater]] that reached a depth of eleven [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in some areas while effectively being an expansive, shallow tidal pool elsewhere.<ref name="GG42" />

F'tral was a tectonically active world, with [[Volcano/Legends|volcanoes]] that were located both undersea and above water contributing [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfurous]] fumes to the planet's thin [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-and-[[Carbon dioxide/Legends|carbon dioxide]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. In the regions where F'tral's tectonic plates collided, chains of volcanic islands rose<ref name="GG42" /> that were covered in [[Swamp/Legends|marshes]] and swamplands<ref name="Creatures" /> and featured lush [[Plant/Legends|flora]].<ref name="GG42" />

===Fauna and flora===
{{Quote|With such fierce competition for nutrients, it is little wonder that animal life never developed beyond the insect and reptilian stages on land. Instead, the versatile cephalopods evolved to fill the ecological niche left vacant by the absence of advanced vertebrates.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:Sensor_star.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The sensor star lived on the volcanic islands of F'tral.]]
The soil on the islands was poor in organic enzymes, leading to most F'tral land plants to adopt a [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] or [[Parasite/Legends|parasitic]] lifestyle in order to obtain the necessary [[Protein/Legends|proteins]] and other nutrients. Such flora included the [[fisher plant]], which captured aquatic animals by trapping them in tendrils trailing off the island shores; the [[seducer plant]], which used a sweet smelling nectar to attract prey that it subsequently strangled using its [[Vine/Legends|vines]] and stems; and several types of [[cannibal plant]], which were capable of moving to stationary plants and then either consuming them or feeding on nutrients drawn from their root systems.<ref name="GG42" />

Due to the fierce competition for nutrients among F'tral's plants, the most complex animal life on land were [[Insect/Legends|insects]]<ref name="GG42" /> and [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]]—including [[Snake/Legends|snakes]]—although [[Fish/Legends|fish]] also lived in inland waters and the planet's swamp regions were home to the small five-limbed aquatic creature known as the [[sensor star]].<ref name="Creatures" /> On F'tral, [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] never evolved at all,<ref name="GG41" /> and instead the ecological niche usually occupied by advanced [[vertebrate]]s was filled by muscular, [[Endothermy|warm-blooded]] [[Cephalopod/Legends|cephalopods]], including a [[Iyra precursor species|species]] that eventually gave rise to the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] Iyra. Cephalopods rapidly established themselves as the dominant creatures in F'tral's oceans, with the Iyra in turn eventually becoming the dominant form of cephalopods.<ref name="GG42" />

==History==
{{Quote|He was known as a demanding but fair teacher, and his anecdotes about life as a young Iyra on F'tral provided a distinct (and amusing) break from the pro-Imperial, Human-oriented viewpoint that tends to predominate at the University of Sanbra.|A [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]] note on Tem Eliss|<ref name="GG12">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Tem_Ellis.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Iyra sentientologist Tem Eliss was born on F'tral.]]
The Iyra [[sentientologist]] [[Tem Eliss]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on F'tral at some point at least several [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] prior to<ref name="Journal14">{{JournalCite|14|Alien Encounters (article series)}}</ref> [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].<ref name="Date Batta">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Furthermore, ''Alliance Intelligence Reports'' states that [[Callow Batta]] had been fired from the [[University of Sanbra]] "about a year ago" and therefore around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. Finally, {{JournalCite|14|Alien Encounters (article series)}} establishes that [[Tem Eliss]] was active around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of Batta's removal from the [[University/Legends|university]], meaning [[Gender/Legends|he]] must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] at some point prior to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].</ref> Although Eliss did spend a portion of [[Gender/Legends|his]] youth on his homeworld—later narrating some of his experiences from that [[Time/Legends|time]] period to his students at the [[University of Sanbra]]<ref name="GG12" />—the Iyra was ultimately raised offworld as opposed to on F'tral. As a result, Eliss was not immersed in Iyra culture and came to form friendships with many members of other species, for which he was in turn disliked by the rest of the Iyra society.<ref name="Journal14" />

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Iyra individuals were among the members of several aquatic species that sought refuge from that government on the [[Calamari sector/Legends|Mon Calamari Space]] asylum [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Krinemonen III]].<ref name="SoR">''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]''</ref> The sentientologist [[Obo Rin]] at some point included F'tral and the Iyra in the ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]''. In that work, Rin described the sentient species that he considered to be the most important in the galaxy and of the most interest to the Empire.<ref name="GG42" /> F'tral was also mentioned in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to sensor stars.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Iyra}}
{{Quote|Iyra society evolved technologically because of the Iyra's ability to use their tentacles to create tools and manipulate their environment.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
F'tral was the homeworld of the sentient Iyra species. [[Skeleton/Legends|Boneless]] and sporting at least five [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] as well as a corresponding number of [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], the Iyra were the dominant form of cephalopods on F'tral and lived underwater, although they could also breathe and move outside of water. Their society was organized into a rigid system of castes based on the number of an individual Iyra's tentacles.<ref name="GG42" />

[[File:Iyragg4.jpg|thumb|left|300px|F'tral was the homeworld of the Iyra.]]
The Iyra used the [[Temperature/Legends|heat]] of F'tral's undersea volcanoes to [[Metallurgy/Legends|smelt]] [[Ore/Legends|ores]], which eventually allowed them to develop various advanced technologies, including those necessary for interstellar travel. In order to maintain a [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]] aboard the [[Starship/Legends|starships]] they [[Starshipwright|constructed]]—but not simply fill them with water as that would render the vessels' weight prohibitively high for leaving planetary [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity wells]]—the Iyra filled the ships' cabins with a special mixture of [[Gas/Legends|gases]] rich in water vapor. The vapor allowed the gas mixture to freely transfer through the epidermal cells of the on-board Iyra. In addition, since the stresses caused by the extreme accelerations during spaceflight were dangerous to the boneless bodies of the Iyra, they outfitted their starships with sophisticated [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] manipulation systems that allowed the vessels to maintain a constant gravitational acceleration and compensate any inertial disturbances.<ref name="GG42" />

The Iyra became widespread interstellar [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] who preferred traveling to planets that offered marine docking facilities.<ref name="GG42" /> By the time shortly following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="SWR">[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]</ref> the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] had come to highly value the corrosion-resistant alloys and hydraulic, [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]], and [[nuclear fusion]] technologies the Iyra had developed. However, due to the Iyra's traditional disdain for beings with less than five limbs, they eventually came to be seen by other species as arrogant and pompous.<ref name="GG42" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|The Iyra live in vast, submarine cities on F'tral's ocean bottoms.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
The Iyra of F'tral lived in expansive [[City/Legends|cities]] located on the bottom of the planet's oceans and maintained [[Shipyard/Legends|facilities]] for the construction of starships. Twice a [[Local year|year]], males and females of the same Iyra caste carried out mating rituals at a location known as the [[breeding grounds]].<ref name="GG42" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Gg4-1.jpg|thumb|right|300px|F'tral was introduced in ''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races''.]]
F'tral was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a [[1989]] sourcebook authored by [[Troy Denning]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG41" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], misspelled the planet's name as "F'Tral" and placed it in the [[Tau Sekar sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was never confirmed.<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref>

[[David Plunkett]] provided the first illustration of F'tral in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'',<ref name="Creatures" /> while the first full appearance of the planet came in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]. In the latter work, the image used to depict F'tral was also used to illustrate ten other worlds.<ref name="SWR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the F'tral [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square T-9,<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book later overrode the ''Voyages SF'' placement by establishing that the system was instead located in the Calaron [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />
